name: 2Checkout (Verifone) Payment and Commerce API
what_it_does: Global payment and commerce platform with REST API — enabling digital goods and SaaS companies to accept payments in 180+ countries, manage subscriptions, handle recurring billing, process refunds, and manage product catalogs, with built-in tax compliance, localized checkout in 45+ languages, and an all-in-one merchant of record option for global digital commerce.
use_cases: Processing global digital goods payments via 2Checkout API for software licensing sales
Managing SaaS subscriptions and recurring billing via 2Checkout for global markets
Implementing merchant of record via 2Checkout for outsourced tax/compliance on digital goods
Accepting payments in 180+ countries via 2Checkout for global SaaS expansion
Managing product catalog and pricing via 2Checkout API for multi-currency pricing
not_for: Physical goods with complex shipping/fulfillment (2Checkout is digital goods focused)
Enterprise with very high volume needing bespoke payment infrastructure (use Stripe/Braintree)
Simple US-only payments without subscription management needs (use Stripe for simplicity)
best_when: A software/SaaS company selling digital goods globally needs an all-in-one payment platform — where 2Checkout handles global payment acceptance, subscription management, and can act as merchant of record for international tax compliance.
avoid_when: You primarily sell physical goods, are US-only, or need developer-first payments infrastructure without commerce features.

auth.notes: Two auth methods: OAuth2 (preferred for new integrations) and legacy API key/secret HMAC-SHA256 signature. OAuth2: client credentials grant, Bearer token. Legacy: merchant code + HMAC-SHA256 signature of request parameters. API v6 uses OAuth2 exclusively. Webhooks via Instant Payment Notification (IPN). Separate sandbox environment. Multiple SDK languages. Acquired by Verifone 2020 — rebranded under Verifone.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; sandbox for testing
pricing.notes: Founded 1999 (Avangate acquired 2Checkout brand 2017). Acquired by Verifone 2020. Columbus, Ohio. 50,000+ merchants globally. Originally focused on software/shareware. 180+ countries. 45+ languages. 8 payment methods (cards, PayPal, etc.). All-in-one MoR (2MONETIZE plan): tax collection, VAT, compliance handled by 2Checkout. Subscription management built-in. Chargeback management. Affiliate network. Used by: Bitdefender, ABBYY, Kaspersky, Acronis. EU VAT handling. Global tax compliance in MoR plan.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API v6 with OAuth2. Legacy API uses HMAC-SHA256 signatures. PHP/Python/Ruby/JS/Java/.NET SDKs. Webhooks (IPN). Sandbox environment. PCI-DSS/GDPR compliant. 180+ countries. Merchant of Record option (2MONETIZE plan).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order placement has idempotency via reference number. Subscription management reads idempotent. Refund/cancel operations — verify before retry. Use order reference for deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Two auth systems coexist: OAuth2 (v6) and legacy HMAC-SHA256 (older API versions) — use OAuth2 for new code
Acquired by Verifone 2020 — documentation may reference old Avangate API (largely compatible)
IPN (Instant Payment Notification) webhooks use different auth than REST API — separate HMAC validation
Sandbox requires separate account from production — different merchant code/credentials
2MONETIZE plan (MoR) has different API capabilities vs 2SELL/2SUBSCRIBE — verify plan entitlements
Legacy API uses HMAC-SHA256 signature of sorted parameters — complex to implement correctly

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. PCI-DSS compliant. GDPR compliant. OAuth2 for v6 API. Legacy HMAC-SHA256 (weaker than OAuth2). Payment data never touches merchant server in hosted checkout. IPN webhook validation required. Verifone corporate security standards. US and EU data residency.
